title: Implement email-based password reset and user profile management
markdown:
## Goal

Add email-based password reset (sending a reset link via email) and user profile management (display name, avatar URL) to the wallet server. Today, the password reset flow generates a token but has no email delivery mechanism -- the token is returned in the API response, which is only useful for testing. Profile data is limited to `username` and `createdAt` with no support for user-facing metadata.

## Background

The wallet server (`libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts`) already has password reset token generation and verification:
- `POST /api/v1/auth/credentials/request-password-reset/:appKey` -- creates a reset token and returns it in the response body
- `POST /api/v1/auth/credentials/reset-password/:appKey` -- accepts a token and sets a new password
- The underlying functions (`createPasswordResetToken`, `resetPasswordWithToken`) in `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/auth.ts` handle token storage and validation

What is missing:
1. **Email delivery**: The reset token needs to be emailed to the user, not returned in the API response. This requires an email sending dependency (SMTP or service like SendGrid/SES) and associating an email address with the user account.
2. **Email storage**: User profiles at obfuscated paths (`deriveObfuscatedPath(serverPublicKey, username, "profile")`) store `{ username, createdAt }` but not an email address.
3. **Profile management**: No endpoints exist for updating display name, avatar URL, or other profile fields. No endpoint for reading one's own profile.

The `WalletServerDeps` interface (`libs/b3nd-wallet-server/types.ts`) already supports dependency injection for `storage`, `logger`, `fetch`, and `credentialClient`. An `emailSender` dependency would follow the same pattern.

The `interfaces.ts` file defines `FileStorage`, `Logger`, `HttpFetch`, and `Environment` abstractions. An `EmailSender` interface belongs here.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `EmailSender` interface defined in `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/interfaces.ts` with `sendEmail(to, subject, body)` method
- [ ] `ConsoleEmailSender` default implementation that logs emails to console (for development)
- [ ] `WalletServerConfig` updated with optional `emailSender` and `emailFrom` fields
- [ ] `createUser()` in `auth.ts` accepts and stores an optional `email` field in the profile
- [ ] `request-password-reset` endpoint sends email with reset link instead of returning token in response
- [ ] `request-password-reset` endpoint returns `{ success: true, message: "Reset email sent" }` without exposing the token
- [ ] New `GET /api/v1/auth/profile/:appKey` endpoint returns the authenticated user's profile (username, email, displayName, avatarUrl, createdAt)
- [ ] New `POST /api/v1/auth/profile/:appKey` endpoint updates profile fields (displayName, avatarUrl) for authenticated user
- [ ] Profile updates are stored at the existing obfuscated profile path with signed+encrypted payloads
- [ ] `WalletClientInterface` in `libs/b3nd-wallet/types.ts` updated with `getProfile()` and `updateProfile()` methods
- [ ] Existing password reset tests continue to pass (token-based reset still works for server-to-server use)
- [ ] New tests for email delivery mock, profile read, and profile update

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/interfaces.ts` to understand existing DI abstractions (`Logger`, `FileStorage`, `HttpFetch`).

2. Add `EmailSender` interface and `ConsoleEmailSender` to `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/interfaces.ts`:
   ```typescript
   export interface EmailSender {
     sendEmail(to: string, subject: string, htmlBody: string): Promise<void>;
   }

   export class ConsoleEmailSender implements EmailSender {
     async sendEmail(to: string, subject: string, htmlBody: string): Promise<void> {
       console.log(`[EMAIL] To: ${to}, Subject: ${subject}`);
       console.log(`[EMAIL] Body: ${htmlBody}`);
     }
   }
   ```

3. Update `WalletServerConfig` and `WalletServerDeps` in `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/types.ts`:
   - Add `emailSender?: EmailSender` to `WalletServerDeps`
   - Add `emailFrom?: string` to `WalletServerConfig`
   - Add `passwordResetUrlTemplate?: string` to `WalletServerConfig` (e.g., `"https://myapp.com/reset?token={token}&username={username}"`)

4. Update `createUser()` in `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/auth.ts` to accept and store an optional `email` parameter in the profile data. The `PasswordCredentialPayload` in `credentials.ts` should also accept optional `email`.

5. Update the `request-password-reset` route in `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts`:
   - Look up the user's email from their profile
   - If email exists and `emailSender` is configured, send the reset link via email
   - Construct the reset URL using `passwordResetUrlTemplate`
   - Return generic success message without exposing the token
   - If no email sender configured, fall back to current behavior (return token) for backward compatibility

6. Add profile endpoints in `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts`:
   - `GET /api/v1/auth/profile/:appKey` -- verify JWT, read profile from obfuscated path, decrypt, return fields
   - `POST /api/v1/auth/profile/:appKey` -- verify JWT, read existing profile, merge updates, re-encrypt, write

7. Update `WalletClientInterface` in `libs/b3nd-wallet/types.ts` and implement in `libs/b3nd-wallet/client.ts`:
   - `getProfile(appKey: string): Promise<UserProfile>`
   - `updateProfile(appKey: string, updates: ProfileUpdate): Promise<void>`

8. Add types: `UserProfile { username, email?, displayName?, avatarUrl?, createdAt }` and `ProfileUpdate { displayName?, avatarUrl? }`

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/interfaces.ts` -- Add `EmailSender` interface and `ConsoleEmailSender`
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/types.ts` -- Add email config fields to `WalletServerConfig` and `WalletServerDeps`
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/auth.ts` -- Update `createUser()` to store email in profile
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/credentials.ts` -- Update `PasswordCredentialPayload` to accept optional email
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts` -- Update password reset route, add profile endpoints
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/mod.ts` -- Export new types and implementations
- `libs/b3nd-wallet/types.ts` -- Add `UserProfile`, `ProfileUpdate`, and update `WalletClientInterface`
- `libs/b3nd-wallet/client.ts` -- Implement `getProfile()` and `updateProfile()`

## Testing

```bash
deno test libs/b3nd-wallet-server/ --ignore=libs/b3nd-client-localstorage
deno test libs/b3nd-wallet/
```

Specific test scenarios:
- Email sender receives correct arguments when password reset is requested with email on profile
- Password reset falls back to token-in-response when no email sender configured
- Profile GET returns all stored fields
- Profile POST updates displayName and avatarUrl without overwriting other fields
- Profile endpoints return 401 for unauthenticated requests

## Dependencies

No hard prerequisites. This is a self-contained enhancement to the wallet server. The email reset capability unblocks real-world user account recovery.
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Email-based password reset
- [ ] User profile management (display name, avatar)
- [ ] API key authentication (server-to-server)
- [ ] Rate limiting
- [ ] Audit logging
- [ ] Key rotation support
- [ ] Multi-factor authentication
- [ ] WebAuthn/passkey support

---

## B. Economic Layer (Gas & Tokenization)
